content: Most existing explorations of LLM self-reflection mechanisms frame self-reflection as a one-time perspective correction step, while Self-Refine demonstrates that iterative unguided self-feedback can boost output quality without extra training. We can hypothesize that stacking multi-perspective reflection checks from LLM self-reflection mechanisms into each iteration of the Self-Refine framework will produce significantly better output quality than either approach alone, even without any supervised training data or external reward signals.
